title: Jordan Advances Green Hydrogen Strategy to Become Regional Energy Hub
content: The Ministry of Energy and Mineral Resources is finalizing a comprehensive national strategy, developed in collaboration with USAID, to position Jordan as a regional hub for green hydrogen production and export. Aligning with King Abdullah IIâ€™s directives, the initiative focuses on attracting investments in green hydrogen, green ammonia, and methanol. The Ministry has already formalized 13 Memoranda of Understanding (MoUs) with international companies and is revising legislative frameworks to better integrate renewable energy. Parallel efforts include modernizing infrastructure through smart grids and metering, expanding the Risha gas and Hamzah oil fields, and advancing electrical interconnections with Egypt, Saudi Arabia, and Iraq. Despite acknowledging challenges such as financial sustainability and technical constraints, the strategy aims to diversify Jordanâ€™s energy portfolio and support global climate goals.
author: Analysts Account
